column1: # Mycelium task-id -> GitHub issue map (created 2026-06-08 via the GitHub MCP server).
#   - issue_number : use for "blocked by" issue-dependency links and human reference.
#   - issue_db_id  : the issue's REST database id; pass as sub_issue_id to sub_issue_write
#                    (sub-issue linking takes the ID, NOT the number).
# Repo: tzervas/mycelium. Milestones are NOT yet assigned (see gh-bootstrap-local.sh).
# Phase 3 (M-3xx build tasks) — created 2026-06-15 from issues.yaml; sub-issues of epics E3-1..E3-7 (#35-41).
# Phase 4 (ABI + hot-inject + AOT-fragment + mutual recursion) — created 2026-06-16; gated on ADR-016/017 (Accepted).
# Phase 4 (M-35x RFC-0008 integration) + Phase 7 (RFC-0008 runtime) — created 2026-06-16.
# M-361 decomposition (M-364..M-368) staged in issues.yaml 2026-06-16; numbers assigned on the next
# gh-sync-all.sh run at the Phase-8 gate (sub-issues of M-361 #132).
# Resolved 2026-06-16: created via gh-sync-all.sh and wired as sub-issues of M-361 (#132); numbers below.
#             (folded; issues kept OPEN until the M-361 gate). Mapping rows above are unchanged (append-only).
# 2026-06-17: RFC-0016 ratification follow-ons created (DN-07 pass). Append-only batch.
# 2026-06-19: docs-currency wave (M-371 navigational docs + doc-currency gate) + fmt->io canonical-
#             JSON delegation (M-372). Created via the GitHub MCP server; real db-ids (append-only).
# 2026-06-22: e7l / Phase-6 gap-closure epics + children, looked up live via the GitHub MCP
#   (search_issues / list_issues on tzervas/mycelium). Append-only; the issue_db_id is the
#   sub_issue_id sub_issue_write takes (NOT the number). The E7-1/E7-2 epic BODIES enumerate their
#   children (the relationship source of truth). LIVE sub-issue links performed this session:
#     E7-1 (#316) ← M-656 (#315) and M-658 (#317)  [both status:done; verified via get_sub_issues].
#   The remaining E7-1 children (M-657, M-659..664) and the E7-2 children (M-665..668) are
#   relationship-recorded in issues.yaml (epic:) and are a maintainer/token follow-up to link live
#   (gh-issues-sync.py is script-ready; this env's MCP set has sub_issue_write but no Projects-v2).
#   HONESTY (VR-5/G2): only the four db-ids below were VERIFIED from a live issue read; the other
#   e7l children's NUMBERS were confirmed from list_issues but their db-ids were NOT fetched — they
#   are intentionally omitted (a db-id is never guessed from a sequential pattern). Run a live
#   issue read (or `--use-api`) to fill them before sub_issue_write. Number-only rows note the gap.
# ISSUE numbers verified (list_issues 2026-06-22, the phase:5 open set); db-ids NOT yet fetched —
# E7-2 children's ISSUE numbers were NOT enumerated this session (M-665..668) — do not confuse with
# their LANDING PR numbers in pr-index.json (M-665→PR#332, M-666→PR#342 are PRs, not issues). Look
# up the issue numbers + db-ids live before linking them under E7-2 (#324).
# 2026-06-23 (M-675): FULL idmap reconcile against live GitHub. Every remaining issues.yaml id
#   (the Phase-3.5/5/6/7/8 backlog + the e7l/u78 epics E3-8/E3-9/E4-1/E5-1/E6-1/E8-1) mapped
#   id -> number -> db_id. The db_ids are FETCHED LIVE (GitHub search API `id` field via the MCP
#   server), never guessed (G2/VR-5) — this RESOLVES the "db-ids NOT yet fetched" gap the
#   2026-06-22 note above flags (M-657 / M-659..664 / M-649 / M-673 / M-674 now carry real db_ids).
#   Cross-validated this run: every PRE-EXISTING row above matches live GitHub (0 mismatches); no
#   duplicate task-id and no duplicate issue-number across the whole map; all 209 issues.yaml ids
#   are now covered. Rows below sorted by issue number. Append-only (rows/notes above unchanged).
# NOTE (data-quality, not acted on here — needs a write-scoped maintainer run): GitHub carries 4
#   DUPLICATE issues from a 2026-06-16 gh-sync-all.sh re-run — #126/#127/#128/#129 duplicate the
#   canonical M-302/#87, M-330/#97, M-342/#104, M-348/#110 (mapped far above). The idmap anchors
#   each task-id to its canonical (earliest) issue; the duplicates are left to a maintainer close,
#   not mapped (mapping a task-id to two numbers would break the id<->number invariant).
# 2026-06-23 (M-675, tul): SECOND idmap reconcile pass — the Phase-5/6/7/8 backlog + gap-closure
#   epics created AFTER the 2026-06-23 pass above (issues #404-485). Every id below mapped
#   id -> number -> db_id with db_ids FETCHED LIVE (the GitHub MCP `search_issues` `id` field —
#   `issue_read`/`list_issues` strip the REST id; `search_issues` exposes it), never guessed
#   (G2/VR-5). Cross-validated this run: all 209 PRE-EXISTING rows still match live GitHub
#   (0 mismatches in number AND db_id); the 4 known duplicate task-ids (M-302/330/342/348) still
#   anchor to their canonical (lower) number; no duplicate task-id, no duplicate issue-number across
#   the whole map. Rows below sorted by issue number. Append-only (rows/notes above unchanged).
# FLAG (authored-ahead, NOT in this map — never invented; G2): 11 issues.yaml ids have NO live GitHub
#   issue yet — M-714 M-715 M-716 M-717 M-718 M-719 (epic E13-1 children) and
#   M-725 M-726 M-727 M-728 M-729 (epic E15-1 children). A prior `--issues` create run materialized
#   They are a `gh-issues-sync.py --issues` create-if-absent follow-up (owned by the issue-creating
#   flow, not tul, which maps ids<->live issues and does not create/re-status). A number cannot be
#   mapped for a non-existent issue (a number-only/db-id-only row would still be a guess) — so they
#   stay uncovered + flagged here until created. After creation: 281/281 issues.yaml ids covered;
#   currently 270/281 (all 11 gaps are exactly these authored-ahead ids).
